inputs.label: Part Thickness
inputs.businessContext: Maximum wall thickness of the plastic part. Critical for cooling time calculation. Based on ISO 294-4 and standard injection molding handbooks.

inputs.label: Thermal Diffusivity of Material
inputs.businessContext: Material property; typical values: PP ~0.08, ABS ~0.1, PC ~0.12. Source: material datasheets.

inputs.label: Mold Temperature
inputs.businessContext: Temperature of the mold surface. Affects cooling rate. Typical range 20-80°C for amorphous polymers.

inputs.label: Melt Temperature
inputs.businessContext: Temperature of the molten plastic entering the mold. Based on material supplier recommendations.

inputs.label: Ejection Temperature
inputs.businessContext: Temperature at which part can be ejected without deformation. Typically 20-40°C below glass transition temperature.

inputs.label: Injection Time
inputs.businessContext: Time to fill the mold cavity. Depends on machine and part geometry.

inputs.label: Packing Time
inputs.businessContext: Time for holding pressure after filling to compensate shrinkage.

inputs.label: Mold Open/Close Time
inputs.businessContext: Time to open and close the mold. Depends on machine speed.

inputs.label: Ejection Time
inputs.businessContext: Time to eject the part from the mold.

inputs.label: Target Cycle Time
inputs.businessContext: Desired cycle time for production planning.

inputs.label: Material Cost per kg
inputs.businessContext: Cost of plastic material per kilogram.

inputs.label: Machine Hourly Rate
inputs.businessContext: Cost per hour to run the injection molding machine.

inputs.label: Number of Cavities
inputs.businessContext: Number of parts produced per cycle.

inputs.label: Scrap Rate
inputs.businessContext: Percentage of parts rejected. Typical range 1-5%.

inputs.label: Machine Power
inputs.businessContext: Power consumption of the injection molding machine.

rules: ejectionTemperature must be less than meltTemperature
moldTemperature must be less than ejectionTemperature
cycleTimeTarget must be greater than sum of injectionTime, packingTime, coolingTime, moldOpenCloseTime, ejectionTime

hiddenLossDrivers: coolingTime > cycleTimeTarget * 0.5 ? 'Cooling time is too high; consider reducing part thickness or increasing thermal diffusivity.' : ''
scrapRate > 5 ? 'Scrap rate exceeds 5%; investigate process parameters or material quality.' : ''
cycleTime > cycleTimeTarget * 1.2 ? 'Cycle time exceeds target by more than 20%; optimize injection or cooling parameters.' : ''
suggestedActions: If cooling time is high, consider using a material with higher thermal diffusivity or reducing part thickness.
Optimize mold temperature and cooling channel design to reduce cooling time.
Reduce injection and packing times if possible without affecting quality.
Increase cavity count to improve productivity.
Implement preventive maintenance to reduce scrap rate.
